title: activity — tenancy hooks
description: Per-tenant lifecycle callbacks (#[AsTenancyHook]) this module contributes. One hook: `ActivityBatchFinalizerHook` — closes any open activity batch when the tenant context tears down (typically on Octane worker request-end), so orphan batches from crashed requests are much rarer.

hooks.name: ActivityBatchFinalizerHook
hooks.purpose: Fires when the tenant context tears down (typically end of an Octane request cycle OR the end of a queued job that ran inside a tenant context). If an activity batch is still open (ActivityBatchStarted fired but ActivityBatchCompleted did not), close it now and emit ActivityBatchCompleted. This significantly reduces orphan-batch volume — the nightly ReconcileActivityBatchesJob only picks up genuinely crashed requests, not clean tear-downs.

notes: The hook fires on tenant tear-down, NOT on Octane request tear-down directly. Reason: a single Octane request may swap tenant context multiple times (impersonation flows, cross-tenant support tools). Firing per-tenant instead of per-request means each tenant gets a clean batch close.
Feature-flag gated (`activity.batch-collapse`) so ops can disable batch collapsing during incidents. When off, every activity row surfaces individually on the feed (no batch collapse rendering).
